sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 3 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: LEFT, LEFT, LEFT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 248, level 2.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Green 3 at (45-50, 30-35), still overlapping purple vertical strip. Target Dark outline 3 at (41-48, 5-12). Need to move LEFT but LEFT only works from bottom.
- Plan: Try LEFT from current position to see if it works. If not, try moving DOWN to bottom and RIGHT to clear purple strip, then find another way.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 164 actions; a strong score needs about 109 or fewer. You are 1.5x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Green 3 is still at (45-50, 30-35) after 3 LEFT actions. LEFT doesn't work from rows 45-50, only from the bottom.
So the only way to move LEFT is from the bottom (rows 57-62). But when Green 3 was at the bottom, it got stuck at cols 30-35 because it was blocked by the purple vertical strip.
Let me think about this differently. The purple vertical strip is at cols 32-34. Green 3 is 6 columns wide. When Green 3 is at the bottom and moves LEFT, it stops at cols 30-35, which overlaps with the purple strip.
Maybe I need to move Green 3 to a position where it's completely to the right of the purple strip, then move LEFT from the bottom. But Green 3 is already at cols 30-35, which overlaps with the purple strip.
Wait, maybe I need to move Green 3 RIGHT first, to get it completely to the right of the purple strip, then move DOWN to the bottom, then LEFT.
Green 3 is at (45-50, 30-35). If I move RIGHT, it will go to cols 33-38, then 36-41, etc. But the purple strip is at cols 32-34, so moving RIGHT will eventually get Green 3 completely to the right of the purple strip.
Let me try:
1. Move Green 3 RIGHT to get it to cols > 34
2. Move Green 3 DOWN to the bottom
3. Move Green 3 LEFT to cross the purple strip
But earlier, when Green 3 was at the bottom and moved LEFT, it stopped at cols 30-35. So even if I move it RIGHT first, it will still stop at cols 30-35 when moving LEFT.
Hmm, this is a problem. Let me think about why Green 3 stopped at cols 30-35 when moving LEFT from the bottom.
Actually, looking at the earlier history, Green 3 moved from (57-62, 42-47) to (57-62, 30-35) after 5 LEFT actions. That's 12 columns in 5 actions. Then, after 10 more LEFT actions, it stayed at (57-62, 30-35). So it stopped at cols 30-35.
The purple vertical strip is at cols 32-34. Green 3 is at cols 30-35, which overlaps with the purple strip (cols 32-34). So Green 3 is blocked by the purple strip.
Maybe the issue is that Green 3 is too wide (6 columns) to fit through the gap. The purple strip is 3 columns wide, and Green 3 is 6 columns wide. So Green 3 can't pass through the purple strip.
Wait, but the purple strip is at cols 32-34, which is 3 columns. The gap to the left of the purple strip is cols 0-31, and the gap to the right is cols 35-63. Green 3 is 6 columns wide, so it can fit in either gap.
But when Green 3 is at cols 30-35, it overlaps with the purple strip (cols 32-34). So Green 3 is partially in the left gap (cols 30-31) and partially in the right gap (cols 35), with the middle part (cols 32-34) overlapping the purple strip.
Maybe I need to move Green 3 to a position where it's completely in the right gap (cols > 34), then move LEFT from the bottom. But when Green 3 moves LEFT from the bottom, it will eventually reach cols 30-35, where it overlaps with the purple strip, and then it will stop.
So the issue is that Green 3 can't cross the purple strip by moving LEFT from the bottom. It gets stuck at cols 30-35.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe I need to use a different approach to move Green 3 to the left side.
Actually, wait. Looking at the board layout again:
- The purple vertical strip is at cols 32-34, rows 0-62.
- The purple horizontal strip is at rows 27-29, cols 0-63.
- Dark outline 3 is at rows 41-48, cols 5-12, which is in the bottom-left quadrant.
- Green 3 is at rows 45-50, cols 30-35, which is in the bottom-right quadrant, overlapping the purple vertical strip.
Maybe I need to move Green 3 UP, above the purple horizontal strip, then LEFT, then DOWN.
But earlier, LEFT only worked from the bottom. So that won't help.
Actually, let me reconsider. Maybe LEFT works from any row, but only when the green is not overlapping with the purple strip. Let me try moving Green 3 RIGHT first, to get it completely to the right of the purple strip, then try LEFT.
Green 3 is at (45-50, 30-35). If I move RIGHT 2 actions, it will go to cols 36-41 (assuming 3 cols/action for RIGHT). Then it will be completely to the right of the purple strip (cols 32-34).
Then I can try LEFT to see if it moves.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Green 3 at (45-50, 30-35), overlapping purple vertical strip. LEFT doesn't work from this row. Need to get Green 3 completely to the right of purple strip, then try LEFT.
Plan: Move Green 3 RIGHT to clear purple strip, then try LEFT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move Green 3 RIGHT to clear purple strip, then try LEFT
result = action(['RIGHT'] * 3)
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] == 32:
        print(f"Green 3: bound={n['boundary']}")
# Now try LEFT
result = action(['LEFT'] * 3)
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] == 32:
        print(f"Green 3 after LEFT: bound={n['boundary']}")
print(f"  game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
